title: Psalms Chapter 20 - Livelihood and Salvation in Times of Trouble

sections.opening: Sit, my child, the candle flickers and the night is wrapped in prayer. Today we meet Chapter 20 of Psalms - a chapter especially meant for livelihood and salvation in times of trouble. This is a chapter that touches the heart of anyone going through financial or spiritual crisis. King David wrote it as a prayer for times of distress, when the heart cries out "May the Lord answer you in the day of trouble" - a request that is always answered in its time. In the previous lesson we learned about the sacred connection between Torah and nature in Chapter 19, how "the heavens declare the glory of God" and how Torah "restores the soul." Now we cross a bridge from divine beauty to earthly needs - for livelihood too is an expression of supreme grace.
sections.theoryA:
How Does Psalms Chapter 20 Bring Livelihood and Salvation?

Chapter 20 of Psalms is a special psalm intended for salvation in times of trouble and for livelihood. Tradition teaches that this is a chapter with great power to bring financial and spiritual abundance to life. The chapter opens with the words "May the Lord answer you in the day of trouble" - a promise that God answers in every difficult situation, especially when a person is in financial or spiritual distress.

The primary virtue of this chapter is for blessed livelihood. When a person is in financial crisis, has no work, or money is insufficient - Chapter 20 is considered one of the strongest chapters for bringing salvation. The sages teach that one should persevere in reading it especially in times of financial difficulty, as it opens the gates of heaven for abundance.

The chapter mentions the importance of the Holy Temple - "May He fulfill all your petitions" means that God will fulfill all your requests for good. Especially requests related to livelihood and practical life. The words "May He remember all your meal offerings" teach that every giving and charity you have done returns to you in double blessing.

The special connection of Chapter 20 to money and blessing stems from its structure. It begins with a request for salvation ("May the Lord answer you in the day of trouble"), continues with prayer for blessing ("May He give you according to your heart"), and ends with complete trust ("Our King will answer us on the day we call"). This is a complete path from trouble to abundance, from request to attainment, from difficulty to blessing.

practice1.instruction: Read Psalms Chapter 20 three times, the third time aloud. Then, write on paper one specific request related to livelihood or financial salvation that you need now. Place the paper under the Book of Psalms for 7 days.

sections.theoryB:
When Do We Say Psalms Chapter 20 and How Is It Connected to the Month of Adar?

Chapter 20 is especially connected to the month of Adar (sign of Pisces), which is a month of abundance and blessing. The sages established that in the month of Adar one should increase reciting Chapter 20 especially, because this is the month when "when Adar enters, joy increases." This joy is connected not only to Purim but to all the blessings that descend in this month.

The sign of Pisces represents abundance, fertility and multiplication - exactly like the blessing that Chapter 20 brings. Fish swim in the great sea, like the abundance that God pours upon creation. Therefore in the month of Adar there is special power to read Chapter 20 and request livelihood and abundance.

In Kabbalah, Chapter 20 is connected to the sefirah of Yesod, which is the sefirah of blessing and abundance flowing to our world. Yesod is the channel through which all the supernal blessings descend below. When we read Chapter 20, we open this channel and invite blessing into our lives.

In numerology, the number 20 is a number of completion and return. It is composed of 2 (duality, partnership) and 0 (infinity, potential). Together they create a number of infinite abundance that comes through partnership with the Creator. This is exactly what Chapter 20 brings - partnership with God that brings infinite abundance.

practice2.instruction: Check which Hebrew month we are currently in. If it's Adar - read Chapter 20 every day this week. If another month, find in the monthly chart which chapter suits the current month, and read it too alongside Chapter 20. Write in a notebook whether you felt a change in energy or financial situation.

sections.deepDive:
What's Not Written in Books: The Hidden Virtue of "Day of Trouble"

Here's what regular books don't reveal: The words "May the Lord answer you in the day of trouble" don't only refer to trouble that already exists, but to a specific day of the week called "the day of trouble" - this is Wednesday. In Kabbalah, Wednesday is connected to Netzach, and sometimes its energies create difficulty or resistance.

The hidden sages teach that whoever reads Chapter 20 on Wednesday, especially in the fourth hour on Wednesday (around 10 AM), opens a special gate of salvation. This is the time when the day of trouble becomes a day of salvation. The meticulous ones say Chapter 20 four times on Wednesday - once for each direction, to bring salvation from all directions.

Another secret: The word "trouble" (tzarah) in gematria equals the word "spirit" (ruach). That is, the trouble we experience is actually a spirit that comes to move us from place to place, state to state. When we understand this, trouble becomes a tailwind that pushes us forward to greater salvation.

questions.question: What is the primary virtue of Psalms Chapter 20?
options: Livelihood and salvation in times of trouble
questions.explanation: Chapter 20 is especially intended for livelihood and salvation in times of trouble, as written 'May the Lord answer you in the day of trouble'

questions.question: To which Hebrew month is Chapter 20 especially connected?
questions.explanation: Chapter 20 is connected to the month of Adar (sign of Pisces), the month of abundance and blessing and 'when Adar enters, joy increases'

questions.question: On which day of the week is there special power to read Chapter 20?
questions.explanation: Wednesday is called 'the day of trouble' in Kabbalah, and therefore reading Chapter 20 on this day opens a special gate of salvation

questions.question: To which sefirah is Chapter 20 connected in Kabbalah?
questions.explanation: Chapter 20 is connected to the sefirah of Yesod, which is the channel through which supernal blessings descend to our world

questions.question: What does the word 'trouble' equal in gematria?
questions.explanation: The word 'trouble' equals the word 'spirit' in gematria - trouble is a spirit that pushes us toward greater salvation

closing.summary: We learned that Chapter 20 of Psalms is a powerful psalm for livelihood and salvation in times of trouble. We discovered its connection to the month of Adar and the sign of Pisces, and the special power of reading it on Wednesday. We understood how it works through the sefirah of Yesod to bring blessing to our world.
closing.wisdom: May the Lord answer you in the day of trouble - every trouble is an invitation to greater salvation.
closing.nextPreview: In the next lesson we will meet Chapter 21 - the chapter of blessing and success, and learn how to continue from salvation to permanent and lasting blessing.

faq.question: How many times a day should one read Psalms Chapter 20?
faq.answer: For livelihood - once a day. For urgent distress - 3 times. On Wednesday - 4 times for all directions.

faq.question: Is there a special time of day to read Chapter 20?
faq.answer: The best time is in the morning before starting the day, or on Wednesday in the fourth hour (around 10 AM).

faq.question: What's the difference between Chapter 20 and Chapter 23 for livelihood?
faq.answer: Chapter 20 is intended for salvation in trouble and urgent situations, Chapter 23 ('The Lord is my shepherd') is intended for steady livelihood and daily security.

faq.question: Can women read Chapter 20 during menstruation?
faq.answer: Yes, there's no restriction. Psalms may be read at any time and in any state, it's not related to purity or impurity.
